They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.

140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).

144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.

56	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
57	
58	Given a commutative square
59	```
60	  X' --g'--> X
61	  |f'        |f
62	  v          v
63	  S' --g---> S
64	```
65	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
66	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
67	
68	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
69	```
70	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
71	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
72	```
73	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
74	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
75	commutativity of the square.
76	
77	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
78	base-change diagram. -/
79	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
80	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
81	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
82	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
83	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
84	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
85	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
86	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)

  Read on the global sections over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) through the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward
  dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} via
  \texttt{gammaPushforwardTildeIso}/\texttt{gammaPushforwardIso}), with the codomain pinned
  by the codomain read (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}), the map
  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the canonical \(R\)-linear map
  \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) — i.e.
  the restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) of the unit value of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read,
        lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
  The generic-square legs are identified with the \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of the tensor
  inclusions \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) and \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} (through \texttt{pullbackSpecIso}). Under
  this identification the \((g')\)-unit factor of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) becomes the
  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit, whose section value is the algebraic unit
  \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}; the three pushforward coherences
  (\(\operatorname{pushforwardComp}\), \(\operatorname{pushforwardCongr}\)) merely rebracket
  the iterated pushforward \(f_*\) along the two factorizations
  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite, which on global sections is
  the functoriality of restriction of scalars and so introduces no new content. Composing,
  the \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the
  \(\operatorname{restrictScalars}\psi\) of the codomain-read of \(\eta_M\), namely
  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\); this is the ``\(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R'
  \otimes_R A)\), \(\mathcal F'\) is \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the
  source.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Seam 3: the $(g^* \dashv g_*)$ transpose of the comparison on sections]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'
  % (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the "boils down to the equality" step
  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L933--938).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the equality
  % $$ (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M $$ as $R'$-modules."
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
  % LEAN SIGNATURE (LHS elaboration-checked). The factorization is Mathlib's
  %   `Adjunction.homEquiv_counit`:
  %     pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) f' g' h.w (tilde M)
  %       = (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).map inner
  %          ≫ (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map ψ)).counit.app Z
  %   (`inner`, `Z`, `f'`, `g'` as in lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex). The section-level
  %   content, the genuine coherence, is the equality of `ModuleCat R'` morphisms:
  %     {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
  %       (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
  %         (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
  %           ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).map inner ≫
  %             (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map ψ)).counit.app Z) ≫
  %         (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
  %         = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv
  By the counit formula for the \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-adjunction
  (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\); Mathlib's \texttt{Adjunction.homEquiv\_counit}),
  \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap} factors as \(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by the
  counit, with no opaque adjunction transpose remaining. On the global sections over
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R'\), conjugated by the domain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and codomain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}), this transpose is the extension of scalars
  along \(\psi : R \to R'\) (the \(R'\)-base change) of the \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section
  reading \(\rho\) of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) supplied by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}; on the generator \(r' \otimes m\) it returns
  \((1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), which is exactly the inverse of the regrouping isomorphism
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
  The factorization \(\Gamma(\theta) = \Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ
  \Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\) is the counit form of the adjunction transpose
  (\texttt{Adjunction.homEquiv\_counit}, verified to rewrite
  \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap} on the nose). It remains to read the right-hand factor on
  sections. The pullback dictionary (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}) identifies
  \(g^* = (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)^*\) of a tilde with extension of scalars along \(\psi\), so
  conjugating \(\Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\) by
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\), \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) replaces it by
  \(\operatorname{extendScalars}\psi\) (equivalently \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\))
  applied to the \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading \(\rho\) of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\)
  from Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}. Since \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1)
  \otimes m\), its \(R'\)-base change sends \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1)
  \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\). This is precisely the inverse of the regrouping
  isomorphism (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on generators, and both maps are
  \(R'\)-linear, so they coincide.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
  The argument is carried out \emph{directly on the module of global sections}; no
  abstract adjoint-mate identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By
  hom-extensionality for \(R'\)-linear maps it suffices to check the asserted equality on
  the \(R'\)-module generators \(r' \otimes m\) of the source \(R' \otimes_R M\), and by
  \(R'\)-linearity on the generators \(1 \otimes m\).

  \emph{The base-change map is the base change of the unit.} For a quasi-coherent sheaf
  on an affine scheme the base-change comparison \(\theta\) is, by construction, the
  pullback–pushforward comparison obtained by applying \(g^*\) to the unit of the
  \(\bigl((g')^*, (g')_*\bigr)\)-adjunction; read on global sections through the two tilde
  dictionaries this is precisely the extension of scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of the
  algebraic unit of extension of scalars along \(A \to A \otimes_R R'\). Concretely, the
  unit \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) sends \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1)
  \otimes m\) (the codomain read identifies \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\)
  with \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}),
  and the domain read identifies the source with \(R' \otimes_R M\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}). The base-change map therefore is the
  \(R'\)-linearization \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\,\eta_M\) of \(\eta_M\)
  evaluated through the \(R'\)-action on the target: on the generator \(r' \otimes m\),
  \[
    r' \otimes m \;\longmapsto\; r' \cdot \eta_M(m)
    \;=\; r' \cdot \bigl((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\bigr)
    \;=\; (1 \otimes r') \otimes m ,
  \]
  using that the \(R'\)-action on \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) is restriction of
  scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\).

  \emph{Identification with \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).} The regrouping isomorphism
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} has inverse sending
  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) on generators. This is the same
  assignment as the displayed map above, and both maps are \(R'\)-linear, so they
  coincide. This is exactly the ``boils down to the equality''
  \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M\) of the source, read as an identity of
  \(R'\)-modules; the regrouping isomorphism realizes the equality and the base-change map
  is its inverse. In particular the conjugated map is an isomorphism, with no flatness
  hypothesis used anywhere.
\end{proof}
